data.title: Travel Expert declares final dividend for year ended March 2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited ( (HK:1235) ) has provided an announcement.

Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.01 per share for the financial year ended 31 March 2026. The dividend, subject to shareholder approval on 18 August 2026, will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 25 September 2026 to shareholders registered by the record date of 11 September 2026.

The shares will trade ex-dividend from 07 September 2026, with a book closure period from 09 to 11 September 2026 to determine eligible holders. The announcement underlines the company’s intention to return cash to shareholders, signaling confidence in its financial position despite a competitive travel market environment.

More about Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited

Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited is a Hong Kong-listed travel services company that operates in the tourism and leisure industry. The group focuses on providing travel-related products and services to customers in Asia, leveraging its local market presence and experience in outbound and regional travel arrangements.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$96.87M

data.title: Swift TV Director Boosts Shareholding via Performance Rights Conversion
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Swift Networks Group Limited ( (AU:STV) ) has provided an update.

Swift TV Ltd has disclosed a change in director Brian Mangano’s interests following the conversion of vested performance rights into fully paid ordinary shares. Prior to the change, Mangano held a substantial mix of direct and indirect shareholdings, options, and FY23–FY25 performance rights, reflecting a heavily equity-linked remuneration structure.

On 2 July 2026, 8,445,946 FY23 performance rights vested and were converted at nil cash consideration into the same number of fully paid ordinary shares, increasing Mangano’s indirect shareholding to 12,664,127 shares while reducing his performance rights balance. The transaction, recorded as a conversion of vested rights rather than a market trade, further aligns the director’s incentives with long-term shareholder interests and clarifies the company’s evolving capital and governance profile.

More about Swift Networks Group Limited

Swift TV Ltd, part of Swift Networks Group Limited, operates in the digital media and telecommunications space, providing television and content delivery services. Its offerings typically focus on managed entertainment and connectivity solutions, positioning the company within niche markets such as enterprise, hospitality, and institutional customers.

The company’s securities structure includes fully paid ordinary shares, listed options, and multiple tranches of performance rights tied to financial year targets and vesting timelines. This mix of equity instruments reflects a remuneration and incentive framework aligned with multi-year performance milestones for key executives.

Swift TV’s market presence is supported by significant shareholdings and performance-based equity held by directors, underscoring the alignment of management interests with shareholder value. The firm’s approach to incentivisation and equity conversion is indicative of broader industry practices in ASX-listed technology and media companies.

Average Trading Volume: 7,246,534

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$8.13M

data.title: Kawasaki Heavy Industries to Raise Over ¥190 Billion for Advanced Manufacturing and Hydrogen Transition
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Kawasaki Heavy Industries ( (JP:7012) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Kawasaki Heavy Industries has approved the issuance of new shares and zero-coupon convertible bonds due 2031 and 2033 via international offerings, with the 2033 issue positioned as the world’s first convertible bond aligned with Climate Transition Bond Guidelines. The fund-raising is designed to bolster its financial foundation and flexibility while supporting both near-term growth and long-term strategic investments.

Proceeds of about ¥92.7 billion from the equity offering will be directed to capital expenditures that expand and modernize production bases in Japan, including facilities for commercial aircraft, gas turbines, robotics and next-generation shipbuilding. The investments will emphasize advanced manufacturing technologies such as robotics, physical AI and mass production automation to enhance capacity, efficiency and value-added solutions for customers.

Approximately ¥101 billion from the convertible bond offering will be used by March 2031 to help build a liquefied hydrogen supply chain and further deploy physical AI across operations, including funding commercial-scale demonstration projects through subsidiary Japan Suiso Energy. The move underscores Kawasaki’s ambition to play a leading role in energy security and carbon neutrality, signaling a deeper commitment to transition finance and positioning the company as an early adopter of climate-aligned capital market instruments.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:7012) stock is a Hold
with a Yen3150.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kawasaki Heavy Industries stock,
see the JP:7012 Stock Forecast page.

More about Kawasaki Heavy Industries

Kawasaki Heavy Industries is a diversified Japanese industrial manufacturer active in sectors including aerospace, energy, robotics and shipbuilding. Its primary products range from commercial aircraft and gas turbines to industrial robots and advanced vessels, with a growing focus on next-generation energy carriers such as liquefied hydrogen, LPG and ammonia for global decarbonization markets.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Qoria Shareholders Back Aura Takeover Scheme Ahead of Court Approval
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The latest update is out from Qoria ( (AU:QOR) ).

Qoria shareholders have overwhelmingly approved a scheme of arrangement for Aura Consolidated Group, Inc. to acquire all fully paid ordinary shares in Qoria in exchange for CHESS Depositary Interests representing Aura common stock. The resolution passed at the scheme meeting with 91.49% of shareholders present and voting in favour and 92.54% of total votes cast supporting the transaction.

The scheme remains subject to court approval and satisfaction of remaining conditions precedent, with a second court hearing scheduled for 7 July 2026. If approved, Qoria will lodge court orders with ASIC, its shares will be suspended from ASX trading on 8 July 2026, and implementation is targeted for 17 July 2026, after which Aura CDIs will trade on the ASX and Qoria shareholders will transition to holding Aura securities.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:QOR) stock is a Buy
with a A$0.42 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Qoria stock,
see the AU:QOR Stock Forecast page.

More about Qoria

Qoria Limited, listed on the ASX under ticker QOR, is an Australian company with its head office in Perth, Western Australia. The company’s fully paid ordinary shares are currently traded on the ASX and will be exchanged for CHESS Depositary Interests representing common stock in Aura Consolidated Group under the proposed scheme of arrangement.

Zenith Minerals Limited has reported a change in director Euan Jenkins’ holdings, reflecting the exercise of previously held options into ordinary shares. Horizon Oil Limited ( (AU:HZN) ) has issued an update.

Horizon Oil Limited has completed its off‑market takeover offer for Cue Energy Resources, with the bid closing on 19 June 2026. Following the offer, the company has released an updated distribution schedule of shareholders and a list of its 20 largest holders, outlining the concentration of ownership.

The disclosure shows that the vast majority of Horizon’s issued capital is held by investors with stakes above 100,001 shares, and that a small number of institutional and nominee entities control significant positions. This ownership profile underscores a high degree of shareholder concentration, which may influence future governance dynamics and liquidity in the company’s shares.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

An update from Forrestania Resources Limited ( (AU:FRS) ) is now available.

Forrestania Resources Ltd has confirmed that 518,921 of its listed options, trading under ASX code FRSOA, have lapsed following their scheduled expiry on 30 June 2026. The lapse of these options slightly simplifies the company’s capital structure by reducing the volume of outstanding derivative securities tied to its ordinary shares.

The company had previously notified registered option holders of the impending expiry in May 2026, and the process has now concluded in line with the terms of issue. While the announcement does not indicate any immediate operational changes, the removal of these options may have minor implications for future potential share dilution and could be relevant for existing shareholders assessing capital management.

Candy Club Holdings Ltd. ( (AU:SCP) ) has provided an update.

Scalare Partners Holdings Ltd. has announced a security consolidation affecting its ordinary fully paid shares, restricted ordinary shares, and listed options, with the change subject to shareholder approval on 4 August 2026. Trading in the post-consolidation securities is scheduled to begin on a deferred settlement basis from 6 August 2026, with a record date of 7 August and new securities issued on 14 August, signalling a capital structure reset that may improve trading dynamics and share price optics for investors.

The consolidation, which encompasses both equity and option classes expiring in 2027, is intended to streamline the company’s register and potentially enhance market perceptions of its securities. By tightening the number of securities on issue and aligning all instruments under the revised structure, Scalare is positioning itself for a more efficient capital markets profile ahead of future corporate or investment initiatives.

More about Candy Club Holdings Ltd.

Scalare Partners Holdings Ltd. is an ASX-listed investment and advisory firm that operates through ordinary and restricted securities and listed options.
